name: Coconut Cream Eggs
recipeIngredient: 3 1/4 cups Mounds Sweetened Coconut Flakes (10-oz. package)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together melted butter, corn syrup, vanilla extract, and salt. Gradually add powdered sugar and coconut flakes, beating until blended.
2. Dust a flat surface with additional powdered sugar. Spoon the coconut mixture onto the prepared surface and knead for about 5 minutes. Using 1-1/2 teaspoons of the mixture at a time, shape into egg shapes. Place on wax paper-covered tray.
3. Refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours before dipping into Simple Chocolate Coating. Place coated eggs on wax paper-covered tray. Store in a cool, dry place. Approximately 40 candies.
Simple Chocolate Coating
2 cups (12-oz.) HERSHEY'S Semi-Sweet Chocolate Chips or HERSHEY'S Mini Semi-Sweet Chocolate Pieces
2 tablespoons plus 2 teaspoons Shortening
Instructions for Simple Chocolate Coating:
1. Place chocolate chips and shortening in a medium, heat-proof bowl. In a separate large, heat-proof bowl, put very warm water (100°F to 110°F) to a 1-inch depth.
2. Carefully place the bowl with chocolate into the bowl with water so that the water comes halfway up the side of the chocolate bowl.
3. With a dry rubber scraper, stir the chocolate and shortening constantly until melted and smooth. Do not allow any water to get into the bowl with chocolate.
If the water cools, replace it with very warm water as directed above. Remove the chocolate bowl from the inside water bowl.
4. Using a fork, dip chilled centers into the chocolate mixture one at a time, gently tapping the fork on the edge of the bowl to remove excess chocolate.
5. Invert each coated center onto the prepared tray and swirl a small amount of melted chocolate over the top to cover any fork marks. Let stand until firm.
6. Store candies loosely covered in a cool, dry place. The coating is best for covering 3 to 4 dozen centers.
If water or moisture gets into the chocolate, it can seize and become stiff. As an emergency measure, stir in additional shortening (1/2 teaspoon at a time) until the chocolate is fluid again.
If the chocolate mixture cools below 84°F, return the bowl with chocolate to the bowl with warm water. Chocolate is best for coating between 84°F and 88°F. HERSHEY'S is a registered trademark of Hershey Foods Corporation. Recipe may be reprinted courtesy of the Hershey Kitchens.
